---
title: "NTS Quarto"
---

This is a Quarto website.

# Intro

This Docusaurus manual documents the markup commands supported by the NTS Documents Docusaurus document type.

The manual is divided into five chapters:

- [Chapter 1: Design Items](#chapter-1-design-items)
- [Chapter 2: Coding](#chapter-2-coding)
- [Chapter 3: Media Content](#chapter-3-media-content)
- [Chapter 4: Tables, Menus, and Trees](#chapter-4-tables-menus-and-trees)
- [Chapter 5: Custom Blocks](#chapter-5-custom-blocks)

The manual uses real Docusaurus MDX pages and small custom MDX components to demonstrate how our custom commands should look and behave.

# Chapter 1: Design Items

Design items are used to structure the page and make important information easier to read.

This chapter covers:

- headings
- bullets
- styled text
- notes
- accordions
- columns

## Headings

Headings divide a document into sections.

They also help create the **On This Page** navigation.

```md
# Document title
## Main section
### Subsection
```

Recommended usage:

- Use `#` once for the main title.
- Use `##` for main chapters or sections.
- Use `###` for smaller sections.

## Bullets

Use normal Markdown bullets for lists.

```md
- First item
- Second item
- Third item
```
***Rendered example***:

- First item
- Second item
- Third item

## Bold and italic text

```md
This is **bold text**.
This is *italic text*.
```
